topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The vase sat on the kitchen counter, a garish display of synthetic roses and wilting lilies. I stared at them, the vibrant colors mocking the dull, suffocating gray of my apartment. There was no note, just the silent, insulting offering. My jaw tightened until a dull ache resonated through my molars. I snatched the arrangement, the stems cold and slick against my suddenly clammy palm. Every fiber of my being screamed at the sheer audacity of it. I spun around, my breath catching in a sharp, shallow gasp, and hurled the bouquet against the wall. It hit with a pathetic, muffled thud, the petals scattering like fallen, useless confetti. I couldn't look at the door, couldn't bear to breathe the same stale air any longer.

[story 2]

"Are you serious?" I hissed, the sound brittle and thin, my voice vibrating with barely contained fury. I picked up the flowers, turning them over in my hands as if they were contaminated objects. The cheap plastic wrapping felt offensively light. Who sends this? What twisted game is this? My knuckles were white where I gripped the stems, the tension in my arms so severe I could feel the tendons straining. I paced the length of the living room, my boots clicking sharply on the hardwood floor, each step a deliberate punctuation mark against the silence. I wanted to rip the whole thing apart, to see what cheap paper was hiding inside, to expose the carelessness of the sender.

[story 3]

My eyes narrowed to slits, fixed on the unaddressed arrangement resting on the entryway table. The sheer lack of effort was what truly set my teeth on edge. It was a deliberate slight, a passive-aggressive declaration of some unseen, ridiculous sentiment. I walked over to the table, my movements stiff and jerky. I didn't touch them immediately; instead, I leaned in close, my face inches from the blooms, my nostrils flaring slightly as if trying to inhale some noxious perfume. A low growl escaped my throat, a sound more animal than human. I snatched the flowers up, my grip bruising the stems, and without a word, I marched toward the front door, intending to destroy the evidence of this baffling, infuriating gesture.
